subtitle: Microbes Are a Menace!
summary: Pasteur's old professor Dumas begs him to save the silkworm industry in southern France, devastated by a mysterious disease called pebrine. Though Pasteur knows nothing about silkworms, he goes to Alais and tries to help farmers sort healthy eggs using microscopes. His first method fails spectacularly, but after years of struggle and a crucial experiment by his assistant Gernez proving the globules are living parasites, Pasteur finally devises a method that works. He nearly dies from a stroke along the way but stubbornly returns to finish saving the silkworms.

segments.anchor_sentence: "The silk country of the South is my native country," answered Dumas.

visual.scene_description: Pasteur, knowing nothing about silkworms, arrives in the devastated silk country of southern France with his family and assistants, greeted by bare mulberry trees and desperate farmers.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a neat-bearded man in round spectacles and black frock coat stepping off a horse-drawn carriage with a brass microscope case under his arm, a woman and children behind him, barren mulberry trees lining a desolate southern French village road, crumbling stone terraces, a few gaunt farmers watching from doorways

segments.anchor_sentence: Then Pasteur unlimbered his microscope, before he had got his family settled—he was like one of those trout fishing maniacs who starts to cast without thought of securing his canoe safely on the bank—he unlimbered his microscope, I say, and began to peer at the insides of sick worms, and particularly at these wee globules.

visual.scene_description: Pasteur demonstrating his microscope to skeptical French farmers around a table covered with silkworm moths pinned to small boards, showing them how to check for globules.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a bearded man in round spectacles and black frock coat standing before a brass compound microscope on a wooden farmhouse table, gesturing emphatically at skeptical weathered farmers in work clothes, silkworm moths pinned to small boards beside the microscope, circular inset showing tiny dark round globules among fatty tissue cells

segments.anchor_sentence: But the next spring his bubble burst, alas.

visual.scene_description: Pasteur slumped in an armchair in his cluttered laboratory at night, dictating letters to Madame Pasteur, while shriveled dead silkworms litter the trays on shelves behind him.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a bearded man in round spectacles and rumpled black frock coat slumped wearily in a wooden armchair, a woman in a long dress writing at a desk by oil lamp light, wooden shelves behind them holding trays of shriveled curled silkworms on mulberry twigs, scattered letters on the floor, a brass microscope pushed aside

segments.anchor_sentence: After a series of such weary months, his instinct to do experiments, this instinct—and the Goddess of Chance—came together to save him.

visual.scene_description: Gernez at a microscope late at night in Valenciennes, excitedly observing that the globules multiply inside dying silkworms, proving they are living parasites.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a young man in shirtsleeves leaning excitedly over a brass compound microscope in a dim room lit by a single oil lamp, healthy silkworm cocoons in a tray on one side and shriveled pepper-spotted dead worms on the other, circular magnified inset showing round dark microsporidian spores multiplying densely among tissue cells

segments.anchor_sentence: It was six months before Pasteur was convinced that Gernez was right, but when at last he understood, he swooped back on his work, and once more called the Committee together.

visual.scene_description: Pasteur, partially paralyzed and leaning on a cane, determinedly boarding a train to return south, while in the background happy farmers hold up trays of plump healthy silkworms spinning cocoons.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a bearded man in round spectacles and black frock coat gripping a walking cane with one hand and a train railing with the other, stepping onto a steam locomotive platform with determined expression, split scene showing joyful farmers in the background holding wooden trays of plump silkworms spinning white cocoons on mulberry twigs, green terraced hillside beyond
